name: Santa Fe Flank Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chili mixture:
- 3 Guajillo chiles
- Finely chop stems and seeds
- 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
- 1 tablespoon brown sugar, firmly packed
- 1 teaspoon thyme leaves, dried
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground pepper
2. Place the prepared chiles and enough water to cover them in a 2-quart saucepan.
3. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes. Drain well.
4. Remove stems from the chiles and transfer to a bowl.
5. Mix the chiles with the remaining ingredients (brown sugar, thyme, salt, pepper) until combined.
6. Rub mixture onto both sides of the flank steak (2 lbs).
7. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
8. Preheat oven broiler.
9. Place beef on rack in broiler pan and cook over medium-high heat until browned, about 5 minutes.
10. Turn beef and continue broiling until medium-rare, about 4 to 6 more minutes.
11. Cut beef diagonally across the grain into very thin slices.
